heading: Qualified tax collection contracts

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Nothing in any provision of law shall be construed to prevent the Secretary from entering into a qualified tax collection contract.

children.label: A
children.text: to locate and contact any taxpayer specified by the Secretary,

children.label: B
children.text: to request full payment from such taxpayer of an amount of Federal tax specified by the Secretary and, if such request cannot be met by the taxpayer, to offer the taxpayer an installment agreement providing for full payment of such amount during a period not to exceed 7 years, and

children.label: C
children.text: to obtain financial information specified by the Secretary with respect to such taxpayer,

prohibits each person providing such services under such contract from committing any act or omission which employees of the Internal Revenue Service are prohibited from committing in the performance of similar services,

children.label: A
children.text: having contacts with taxpayers,

children.label: B
children.text: providing quality assurance services, and

children.label: C
children.text: composing debt collection notices, and
permits subcontractors to perform other services only with the approval of the Secretary.

children.heading: Collection of inactive tax receivables
children.label: c Collection of inactive tax receivables

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary shall enter into one or more qualified tax collection contracts for the collection of all outstanding inactive tax receivables.

children.label: i
children.text: at any time after assessment, the Internal Revenue Service removes such receivable from the active inventory for lack of resources or inability to locate the taxpayer,

children.label: ii
children.text: more than 2 years has passed since assessment and such receivable has not been assigned for collection to any employee of the Internal Revenue Service, or

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a receivable which has been assigned for collection, more than 365 days have passed without interaction with the taxpayer or a third party for purposes of furthering the collection of such receivable.

children.heading: Tax receivable
children.label: B Tax receivable
children.text: The term “tax receivable” means any outstanding assessment which the Internal Revenue Service includes in potentially collectible inventory.

children.heading: Certain tax receivables not eligible for collection under qualified tax collections contracts
children.label: d Certain tax receivables not eligible for collection under qualified tax collections contracts

children.text: is subject to a pending or active offer-in-compromise or installment agreement,
is classified as an innocent spouse case,

children.label: A
children.text: deceased,

children.label: B
children.text: under the age of 18,

children.label: C
children.text: in a designated combat zone,

children.label: D
children.text: a victim of tax-related identity theft,

children.label: E
children.text: a taxpayer substantially all of whose income consists of disability insurance benefits under section 223 of the Social Security Act or supplemental security income benefits under title XVI of the Social Security Act (including supplemental security income benefits of the type described in section 1616 of such Act or ), orsection 212 of Public Law 93–66

children.label: F
children.text: a taxpayer who is an individual with adjusted gross income, as determined for the most recent taxable year for which such information is available, which does not exceed 200 percent of the applicable poverty level (as determined by the Secretary),
is currently under examination, litigation, criminal investigation, or levy, or
is currently subject to a proper exercise of a right of appeal under this title.
an amount not in excess of 25 percent of the amount collected under any qualified tax collection contract for the costs of services performed under such contract, and
an amount not in excess of 25 percent of such amount collected to fund the special compliance personnel program account under section 6307.

children.heading: No Federal liability
children.label: f No Federal liability
children.text: The United States shall not be liable for any act or omission of any person performing services under a qualified tax collection contract.

children.heading: Application of Fair Debt Collection Practices Act
children.label: g Application of Fair Debt Collection Practices Act
children.text: The provisions of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( et seq.) shall apply to any qualified tax collection contract, except to the extent superseded by section 6304, section 7602(c), or by any other provision of this title.15 U.S.C. 1692

children.heading: Contracting priority
children.label: h Contracting priority
children.text: In contracting for the services of any person under this section, the Secretary shall utilize private collection contractors and debt collection centers on the schedule required under , United States Code, including the technology and communications infrastructure established therein, to the extent such private collection contractors and debt collection centers are appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.section 3711(g) of title 31

children.heading: Taxpayers in presidentially declared disaster areas
children.label: i Taxpayers in presidentially declared disaster areas

children.text: relief from immediate collection measures by contractors under this section, and
a return of the inactive tax receivable to the inventory of the Internal Revenue Service to be collected by an employee thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: the total number and amount of tax receivables provided to each contractor for collection under this section,

children.label: B
children.text: the total amounts collected (and amounts of installment agreements entered into under subsection (b)(1)(B)) with respect to each contractor and the collection costs incurred (directly and indirectly) by the Internal Revenue Service with respect to such amounts,

children.label: C
children.text: the impact of such contracts on the total number and amount of unpaid assessments, and on the number and amount of assessments collected by Internal Revenue Service personnel after initial contact by a contractor,

children.label: D
children.text: the amount of fees retained by the Secretary under subsection (e) and a description of the use of such funds, and

children.label: E
children.text: a disclosure safeguard report in a form similar to that required under section 6103(p)(5), and

children.label: A
children.text: an independent evaluation of contractor performance, and

children.label: B
children.text: a measurement plan that includes a comparison of the best practices used by the private collectors to the collection techniques used by the Internal Revenue Service and mechanisms to identify and capture information on successful collection techniques used by the contractors that could be adopted by the Internal Revenue Service.
For damages for certain unauthorized collection actions by persons performing services under a qualified tax collection contract, see section 7433A.
For application of Taxpayer Assistance Orders to persons performing services under a qualified tax collection contract, see section 7811(g).
